tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: 23-Year-Old Rings the Bell: Gene Therapy Functionally Cures His Sickle Cell Disease
stories.summary: Daniel Cressy, 23, became Louisiana's first person functionally cured of sickle cell disease through gene therapy, ringing the bell after two years before pursuing his dream of becoming a commercial pilot.

stories.headline: Two Young Men Pull Elderly Couple and Grandson From Burning Home, Worry About the Fence
stories.summary: Mohsin Qayyum, 22, and Mohammed Yusuf Iqbal, 20, pulled an elderly couple and grandson from a burning home in Leeds, with Iqbal's main worry afterward being the fence he'd damaged jumping over it.

stories.headline: Goalkeeper's Greatest Save: Orlando Gill Sacrificed Everything to Save His Son, Then Saved Paraguay at the World Cup
stories.summary: Goalkeeper Orlando Gill gave up his possessions to rescue his son's life, then became Paraguay's penalty shootout hero at the 2026 World Cup.

stories.headline: Maine's Piping Plovers Soar: 185 Nesting Pairs and 207 Chicks—Decades of Dedication Blooming
stories.summary: Maine's piping plovers reached a record 185 nesting pairs this season, with 207 chicks documented and 92 already able to fly, marking decades of conservation triumph.

stories.headline: They Stayed Behind: Couple Spends 14 Hours Daily Feeding 51 Dogs Left in Wildfire Evacuation
stories.summary: Derek and Diedra refused to evacuate without caring for abandoned pets, spending 14 hours daily comforting 51 dogs until families could return.

stories.headline: From the Margins to Glory: Malik Tillman Scores Stunning Free Kick in Torn Boot and Bloody Sock
stories.summary: After years fighting for his spot, US midfielder Malik Tillman scores a breathtaking free kick with a shredded boot to secure World Cup victory.

stories.headline: Scientists Invented a Jacket That Drinks From the Air—and Gives You Clean Water Daily
stories.summary: Engineers created a wearable textile that pulls one to two bottles of clean drinking water daily straight from humid air, meeting WHO standards.

stories.headline: Nine Female White Rhinos Find Hope in Mozambique: Species Climbing Back From the Brink
stories.summary: Sixty-one rhinos relocated to Mozambique's Zinave National Park join a recovering population, with new calves bringing life to a species nearly lost.

stories.headline: From Cali Academy to Mexico's World Cup: Julián Quiñones Writes Immigrant Success Into the Pitch
stories.summary: Julián Quiñones, born in Colombia and raised in a nonprofit academy in Cali, scored and assisted for Mexico against Ecuador, equaling Rafael Márquez and Cuauhtémoc Blanco on Mexico's all-time World Cup scoring list.

stories.headline: One Woman's Litter Dream Blooms Into a Community: Bailey King's Crusade Inspires Dozens
stories.summary: Bailey King's solo passion for cleaning transformed into a thriving neighborhood movement, proving one person's vision can inspire a village.

stories.headline: Scientists Built a Living Cell From Scratch—and It Grows, Replicates, and Divides
stories.summary: Kate Adamala's team built SpudCell, a synthetic cell from scratch that feeds, grows, and replicates like natural life, opening doors to engineered medicine.

stories.headline: 1,300 Rare Silverspot Caterpillars Released Into the Wild: Endangered Butterflies Get a Fighting Chance
stories.summary: Oregon Zoo releases 1,300 rare caterpillars raised with 90% survival rates, giving endangered butterflies hope for wild repopulation.

stories.headline: 3,500 Boreal Toad Tadpoles Hop Home: Largest Release Yet Celebrates Wildlife Comeback Near Leadville
stories.summary: Volunteers and students released 3,500 boreal toad tadpoles—the largest release yet—celebrating their return to alpine wetlands near Leadville.

stories.headline: Solar Reactor That Thinks Like a Plant: No Batteries, Just Brilliant Self-Regulation
stories.summary: Osaka researchers built a sun-powered electrolyzer that manages its own electricity like a living plant, producing fuel without a single battery.
